questions.question: What is heredity?
questions.correct_answer: Transmission of traits from parents to offspring
questions.explanation: Heredity explains how characteristics are passed from one generation to the next through genes.

questions.question: What are variations?
questions.correct_answer: Differences in traits among individuals of the same species
questions.explanation: Variations occur due to genetic recombination and environmental influences and help species adapt.

questions.question: Who is known as the father of genetics?
questions.explanation: Mendel discovered the basic principles of inheritance through experiments on pea plants.

questions.question: What is a gene?
questions.correct_answer: A segment of DNA that controls a specific trait
questions.explanation: Genes are units of heredity that carry information from parents to offspring.

questions.question: What is a monohybrid cross?
questions.correct_answer: A cross involving one pair of contrasting traits
questions.explanation: It studies the inheritance pattern of a single trait between two organisms.

questions.question: State Mendel’s law of dominance.
questions.correct_answer: In a pair of contrasting traits, the dominant trait is expressed in the F1 generation
questions.explanation: The recessive trait remains hidden when the dominant allele is present.

questions.question: What is the F2 phenotypic ratio in a monohybrid cross?
questions.explanation: Three individuals show the dominant trait and one shows the recessive trait in the F2 generation.

questions.question: What are alleles?
questions.correct_answer: Different forms of the same gene
questions.explanation: Alleles occupy the same position on homologous chromosomes and control the same trait.

questions.question: How is sex determined in humans?
questions.correct_answer: By sex chromosomes: XX for females and XY for males
questions.explanation: The chromosome contributed by the sperm determines the sex of the child.

questions.question: Why are acquired traits not inherited?
questions.correct_answer: Because they do not affect the DNA of germ cells
questions.explanation: Traits developed during an organism’s lifetime cannot be passed to the next generation genetically.

notes.title: Heredity
notes.content: The transmission of characteristics from parents to offspring through genes.

notes.title: Variation
notes.content: Differences in traits among individuals of the same species which help in adaptation and evolution.

notes.title: Gene
notes.content: A segment of DNA located on chromosomes that controls specific inherited traits.

notes.title: Monohybrid Cross
notes.content: A genetic cross that studies the inheritance of a single trait.

notes.title: Sex Determination
notes.content: In humans, females have XX chromosomes and males have XY chromosomes.

facts.fact: Gregor Mendel discovered the fundamental laws of inheritance through pea plant experiments.
Genes are located on chromosomes and carry hereditary information.
The F2 phenotypic ratio of a monohybrid cross is 3:1.
Variations are important for the survival and evolution of species.
In humans, the father determines the sex of the child through X or Y chromosomes.
